task: Add social login to existing Node.js + Express + MongoDB backend and update postmain
role: You are an expert Node.js + Express + MongoDB developer.
description: Add social login (Google, Facebook, X/Twitter, Microsoft/Outlook) to an existing backend that already has working email/password login and registration.

step_1_trace_first.instruction: Before writing ANY code, read and fully understand these files in order

User.js: Exact schema fields and their types
Any existing methods on the schema (pre-save hooks, comparePassword, etc.)
authController.js: How the register function is structured
How the login function is structured
How JWT is signed (payload shape, expiry, secret key name)
How errors are returned (status codes, response shape)
How success responses are returned (response shape)
Any try/catch or async wrapper pattern used
authMiddleware.js: How the decoded user is attached to req (req.user or req.userId)
authRoutes.js: How routes are registered (router.post, router.get, etc.)
How middleware is applied to protected routes

step_1_trace_first.rule: Do NOT invent new patterns. Mirror exactly what already exists in every file.

2a_update_user_model.instruction: Add these fields to the existing schema. Do NOT remove or change any existing fields.

new_static_method.name: User.statics.findOrCreateSocialUser
logic: 1. Search by providers.name + providers.providerId → if found, return that user
2. Else search by email → if found, push new provider into providers array, save, return user
3. Else create a brand new user with the provider in providers array
Return the user document in all three cases

2b_create_social_auth_service.instruction: New file. One verify function per provider. Use axios for all HTTP calls.

verifyGoogleToken.throws: Descriptive error if token is invalid

verifyFacebookToken.throws: Descriptive error if token is invalid

verifyTwitterToken.call: GET https://api.twitter.com/2/users/me with header Authorization: Bearer {token}
verifyTwitterToken.throws: Descriptive error if token is invalid

verifyOutlookToken.call: GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me with header Authorization: Bearer {token}
verifyOutlookToken.throws: Descriptive error if token is invalid

rules: Use axios for all HTTP calls
This is a pure service file — no Express req/res inside it
All functions must be async/await — no .then() chains
Add JSDoc comments on every function: @param, @returns, @throws

2c_add_social_login_to_controller.instruction: Add socialLogin function to the EXISTING file. Do NOT change any existing functions.
flow: 1. Validate provider is one of: google | facebook | twitter | outlook → 400 if invalid
2. Call the matching verify function from socialAuthService.js → 401 if token invalid
3. Call User.findOrCreateSocialUser() with the returned profile
4. Sign JWT using the EXACT same pattern as existing login (same payload, secret, expiry)
5. Return success response using the EXACT same shape as existing login response
2c_add_social_login_to_controller.rule: Mirror the exact try/catch pattern already used in authController.js

2d_update_routes.instruction: Add one route to the EXISTING file. Do NOT change any existing routes.

new_route.middleware: none — this is a public route like /auth/login

2d_update_routes.rule: Mirror the exact route registration style already used in the file

step_3_env_variables.note: Google, Facebook, Outlook token verification does not require server-side secrets — the token is verified by calling the provider's public endpoint directly

rules: Use axios for all provider API calls
No passport.js — keep it stateless and simple
All functions must be async/await — no .then() chains
Error handling must mirror the existing pattern in authController.js exactly
No new dependencies except axios (if not already installed)
Mirror variable naming style found in the existing codebase
Mirror the response shape (success/error) already used in the codebase
This is a JavaScript project — no TypeScript
Use JSDoc comments on every new function for type hints
output_order: .env.example — only the new lines to add
output_rules: Full working code — no placeholders except TODO for credentials
Add TODO comments wherever a provider credential must be configured
Do not truncate — output the complete file every time
final_verification_checklist: JWT is signed with the EXACT same payload and secret as existing login
Response shape from socialLogin matches existing login response exactly
No existing register or login function is modified in any way
User.findOrCreateSocialUser handles all 3 cases: existing provider / existing email / new user
POST /auth/social is public — no auth middleware applied
Twitter bearer token is read from process.env.TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN
All provider verify functions throw on invalid token — not return null
socialAuthService.js is a clean service — no Express req/res inside it
